notes: Gravity for STATIC BLOOM. The medium scenario — Friday-night brunch service that mostly holds. The floor is loud but the bloom (the night holding) grows steadily. Pressing BLOOM forward; small DOUBT pulses from the Friday-night noise; STAGNATION rare. The third, inside Nicola, kicks at the scheduled rhythm.

deck.title: the boat groans
deck.flavor: it does not, technically, list. dante checks the levels with his father's spirit level once a month. the groan is part of the room.

deck.title: the brass rail catches the light
deck.flavor: the rail dante installed in the second year. it is, you note crossing the dining room, bright in a way the rest of the boat does not, otherwise, manage.

deck.title: sammy starts a story
deck.flavor: about a regular from '04. he tells it to whoever is closest to the well. the story is, you have heard it before, the kind of story that takes exactly the length of two-thirds of a refill.

deck.title: hector calls an order
deck.flavor: from the pass. two florentines. one steak medium. one steak medium-rare with the sauce on the side. you call back the order to confirm.

deck.title: the third kicks
deck.flavor: small, on the side you have learned is hers. you smooth the apron. the room does not, today, notice. you do.

deck.title: the table 12 wave
deck.flavor: imperious. from across the dining room. you acknowledge with the small half-step. you bring the second pitcher.

deck.title: the fluorescent in the corridor
deck.flavor: downstairs. the long bulb in the back corridor that has, since tuesday, been doing the strobe-flicker thing. you log it. you do not, this hour, go down.

deck.title: the helm light comes on
deck.flavor: at 9:41. mr. d is in the office. you can, through the leaded window, see his shape. the light is on the way he has it on when he is reading.

deck.title: a pitcher runs low
deck.flavor: at table 5. you note from the floor. you head to the well. sammy has the pitcher refilled before you ask.

deck.title: the six-top arrives early
deck.flavor: the private dining party. fifteen minutes ahead of the reservation. you walk them through the partition without registering that they are early.

deck.title: gloria passes with the water
deck.flavor: she sets the glass on the corner of the stand without looking at you. you have, in three years, learned the gesture. you drink half. you stand for one beat.

deck.title: tomás clears a four-top
deck.flavor: in under ninety seconds. plates stacked the way sammy taught him. you note, from the stand, the turnaround.

deck.title: dante descends at 10:14
deck.flavor: the rounds. he walks the floor. he pauses at the maître d' stand. he says: how's tonight. you say: fine, mr. d. he nods.

deck.title: the floor held
deck.flavor: the last seating is at 10:30. nobody has, this evening, complained about anything more substantial than the sauce-on-the-side request. you write the small mark in the reservation page that means clean night.

deck.title: dante closes the helm light
deck.flavor: the window goes dark at 11:32. the rounds are done. the bourbon, half, is finished. the floor is yours to close. the third, inside you, has been still for half an hour.
